text:
Program:The World Today

Presented by

Members of Australia's most-successful Olympic team have returned home today on board flights to cities around the country where they've been greeted by crowds of adoring family, friends and fans.In Sydney they were also met by the Governor General and the Prime Minister, who've paid tribute to their outstanding efforts while wearing the green and gold.

Featured:Samantha Mostyn, Governor-General of AustraliaAnthony Albanese, Prime MinisterJess Fox, Australian SwimmerKayley McKeown, Australian SwimmerBailey Carter, Step-brotherSummer Phillips, SchoolgirlMeg Harris, Australian Swimmer

Dual Paris Olympics gold medal-winning canoeist Jess Fox arriving in Sydney on August 14, 2024.(ABC News: Mary Lloyd)

Rachel Mealey:They were weighed down by gold, silver and bronze, but the aeroplanes carrying Australia's triumphant Olympic heroes have touched down on home soil. The Olympians were greeted by crowds of adoring family, friends and fans. And in Sydney, the Governor-General and the Prime Minister paid tribute to their outstanding efforts in green and gold. Nick Grimm reports.

Announcer:And now for our final huge round of applause, the team that we are welcoming home, would you please put your hands together for the Australian Olympic team.

Nick Grimm:It's been a long journey to get here. 25 hours on a flight from Europe, on top of four years of training, preparation and dedication. And today inside a massive aircraft hangar at Sydney's Kingsford Smith Airport, hundreds of people gathered before dawn for the arrival home of their sporting heroes. The tributes led by the Prime Minister, Anthony Albanese and the Governor-General, Samantha Mostyn.

Samantha Mostyn:As Olympians, you've shown us you're capable of great things. And the standard you set in Paris comes from your courage, your determination, your optimism and your focus. And I just want to say on behalf of all Australians, those qualities are not date stamped. They have no expiry. They're not dimmed by time or circumstance and they'll carry you through your life. You lifted us in Paris.

Anthony Albanese:And for those of us who were back here, staying up late, getting up early, we want you to know that what you have done is inspire us, is give us joy, give us excitement and lifted up our whole nation due to your performance.

Nick Grimm:Those with gold, silver and bronze around their necks and those who also went to Paris and delivered their best, were mobbed by fans seeking selfies with their sporting idols. Among them, dual gold medalist, canoeist Jess Fox. You're the woman of the moment, even the police escorts here are asking for selfies with you.

Jess Fox:Yeah, look, it's pretty overwhelming to get home and see how much love and support there is for our whole team. It's lovely to be able to share this moment with everyone and show the medal and say thank you for the support.

Nick Grimm:The crowd also hearing from swimmer Kayley McKeown, who has come home weighed down by five Olympic medals, two gold, one silver and a couple of bronze.

Kayley McKeown:I'm so excited just to be home, be on Aussie soil and kind of just reap the rewards. Most successful Australian team, so woo, go Australia.

Nick Grimm:The occasion was also an opportunity for Kayley McKeown to be reunited with her stepbrothers, Lachlan and Bailey Carter, decked out in their own green and gold outfits.

Bailey Carter:Yeah, we got the Four and Twenty hats on, some nice green wigs, some Aussie scarves. And I guess our main thing would be these stunning green and gold sequined dresses. They stand out just a little bit.

Nick Grimm:And why is a welcome home like this so important from their perspective, do you reckon?

Bailey Carter:I guess it's part of the whole Olympic experience and they've earned this through their many years of training and it's just a bit of a reward for everything they've done.

Nick Grimm:But many of those who braved the morning drizzle to welcome home the Olympic team were there to simply get close to those they've cheered on from their living rooms over recent weeks, people like schoolgirl Summer Phillips.

Summer Phillips:So I've come along today to support my best friend Jaclyn Barclay. She was the youngest member of the Australian swimming team. She did a phenomenal job in Paris. I haven't seen her in three months because the swimming team went over to the training camp earlier, so I'm really excited to see her and support all of our athletes that have come back.

Nick Grimm:And for Olympians like swimmer Meg Harris, the welcome home ranks right up there with the thrill of her gold and two silver medal haul. Congratulations on your Games performance. Can I ask you, what are your emotions this morning coming home?

Meg Harris:Pretty overwhelming. I'd say this whole experience walking into this room full of people is wholesome. I mean it's just nice that I didn't get to experience this in Tokyo, we went straight into lockdown, but just seeing everyone, like the support, like we felt it from home, all the messages and everything, but to actually come home and experience everyone's family and friends celebrating is exceptional.

Nick Grimm:And I reckon this will just be the start of a lot of celebration and people showing their appreciation for what the entire team achieved over in Paris.

Meg Harris:Yeah, I mean definitely like everyone here deserves it, like the whole team lifted together and like it was an exceptional Games and I just think that everyone here, everyone that did so well, they deserve to enjoy it and they deserve to be like felt like they did.

Rachel Mealey:Olympic champion swimmer Meg Harris ending that report by Nick Grimm.

title: Olympic team returns bearing Gold, Silver and Bronze - ABC listen

keywords: podcast, Sydney, Paris 2024 Olympics, Gold, Silver, Bronze, Kingsford Smith Airport, athletes
